content:
# New Supplier Questionnaire

**Tool ID:** T015-03
**Block:** B015 (Supplier Due Diligence)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L2
**Format:** Assessment
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 20-40 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a system-building tool. It takes your daily practices and turns them into something structured, repeatable, and visible to your team.

A structured way to evaluate capability and progress. Before you place your first order with a new supplier, this questionnaire ensures they meet the food safety, allergen, and traceability standards your cafe requires. It is not about being difficult -- it is about protecting your customers and your business.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Assessment questionnaire for evaluating new suppliers before approval: food safety management system, allergen controls, traceability, recall procedures, and insurance details. Completed before any supplier is added to the Approved Supplier Register (T015-01).

This tool supports **Supplier Due Diligence** (B015) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager (sends to potential supplier and reviews responses)
**Time Required:** 20-40 minutes to review a completed questionnaire
**Frequency:** Once per new supplier, before first order

**What You'll Need:**
- This questionnaire (send to the potential supplier to complete)
- Time to review their responses and supporting documents
- Approved Supplier Register (T015-01) to add them if approved
- Allergen Matrix (T005-01) to cross-reference their allergen information

**Steps:**
1. When a new supplier is proposed, send them this questionnaire
2. Ask them to complete and return with supporting documentation
3. Review their responses against the scoring criteria below
4. Score each area and make an approval decision
5. If approved, add to the Approved Supplier Register (T015-01)
6. If not approved, explain what is missing and give them the opportunity to address gaps
7. File the completed questionnaire in the supplier due diligence folder

---

## Assessment

### Supplier Information

| Field | Supplier Response |
|-------|------------------|
| Company name | _____ |
| Trading name (if different) | _____ |
| Address | _____ |
| Main contact name | _____ |
| Phone | _____ |
| Email | _____ |
| Website | _____ |
| Company registration number | _____ |
| Products you would supply to us | _____ |
| How long have you been trading? | _____ |
| Do you supply other cafes/restaurants? (references available?) | ☐ Yes ☐ No |

### Area 1: Food Safety Management System

| # | Question | Supplier Response | Score |
|---|---------|------------------|-------|
| 1.1 | Do you hold a current food safety certification? (e.g., SALSA, BRC, SQF, ISO 22000, FSSC 22000) | ☐ Yes (type: _____  expiry: _____) ☐ No | GREEN: Yes, current / AMBER: Expired but renewing / RED: No certification |
| 1.2 | What is your most recent food safety audit score / rating? | Score: _____  Date: _____ | GREEN: Grade A/AA or score 90%+ / AMBER: Grade B or 70-89% / RED: Below 70% or no audit |
| 1.3 | Do you have a documented HACCP plan? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / RED: No |
| 1.4 | What is your local authority Food Hygiene Rating? | Rating: _____ (0-5) | GREEN: 5 / AMBER: 4 / RED: 3 or below |
| 1.5 | Do you have documented cleaning schedules and pest control procedures? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / RED: No |
| 1.6 | Are your staff trained in food safety? To what level? | ☐ Level 2 ☐ Level 3 ☐ Other: _____ | GREEN: Level 2+ / AMBER: In-house only / RED: No training |

### Area 2: Allergen Management

| # | Question | Supplier Response | Score |
|---|---------|------------------|-------|
| 2.1 | Can you provide full allergen specification sheets for every product you supply? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes, available / RED: No |
| 2.2 | Do your product labels declare all 14 EU allergens? | ☐ Yes ☐ No ☐ N/A (unpackaged) | GREEN: Yes / AMBER: Partial / RED: No |
| 2.3 | Do you have a procedure for notifying customers of recipe changes that affect allergens? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes, written procedure / AMBER: Verbal only / RED: No |
| 2.4 | Do you have procedures to prevent allergen cross-contact during production? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes, documented / AMBER: Informal / RED: No |
| 2.5 | Will you notify us BEFORE changing any ingredient or recipe? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / RED: No |

### Area 3: Traceability and Recall

| # | Question | Supplier Response | Score |
|---|---------|------------------|-------|
| 3.1 | Can you trace every product back to its source within 4 hours? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / AMBER: Within 24 hours / RED: No |
| 3.2 | Do you have a documented product recall/withdrawal procedure? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / RED: No |
| 3.3 | Have you conducted a recall in the last 2 years? If yes, what happened? | ☐ Yes (details: _____) ☐ No | INFO only -- not scored |
| 3.4 | Do you use batch/lot coding on your products? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / AMBER: Partial / RED: No |

### Area 4: Delivery and Transport

| # | Question | Supplier Response | Score |
|---|---------|------------------|-------|
| 4.1 | Are your delivery vehicles refrigerated for chilled/frozen goods? | ☐ Yes ☐ No ☐ N/A (ambient only) | GREEN: Yes / RED: No (for chilled/frozen) |
| 4.2 | Do you monitor and record vehicle temperatures during transit? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / AMBER: Driver checks only / RED: No |
| 4.3 | Can you confirm delivery windows to avoid peak service times? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / AMBER: Limited flexibility / RED: No |
| 4.4 | Do you separate raw and ready-to-eat goods during transport? | ☐ Yes ☐ No ☐ N/A | GREEN: Yes / RED: No |

### Area 5: Insurance and Legal

| # | Question | Supplier Response | Score |
|---|---------|------------------|-------|
| 5.1 | Do you hold public liability insurance? (minimum 5 million recommended) | ☐ Yes (amount: _____  expiry: _____) ☐ No | GREEN: Yes, 5M+ / AMBER: Yes, below 5M / RED: No |
| 5.2 | Do you hold product liability insurance? | ☐ Yes (amount: _____) ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / AMBER: Included in public liability / RED: No |
| 5.3 | Are you registered with your local authority as a food business? | ☐ Yes ☐ No | GREEN: Yes / RED: No |

### Scoring Summary

| Area | # of GREEN | # of AMBER | # of RED | Overall |
|------|-----------|-----------|---------|---------|
| 1. Food Safety | ___/6 | ___/6 | ___/6 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 2. Allergen Management | ___/5 | ___/5 | ___/5 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 3. Traceability | ___/3 | ___/3 | ___/3 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 4. Delivery | ___/4 | ___/4 | ___/4 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |
| 5. Insurance | ___/3 | ___/3 | ___/3 | ☐ G ☐ A ☐ R |

### Approval Decision

| Decision | Criteria |
|----------|---------|
| **APPROVED** | No RED scores. Predominantly GREEN. Add to Approved Supplier Register (T015-01). |
| **CONDITIONALLY APPROVED** | 1-2 AMBER scores in non-critical areas. Supplier must address gaps within 30 days. Monitor first 3 deliveries closely. |
| **NOT APPROVED** | Any RED score in Areas 1, 2, or 5. Supplier cannot be used until gaps are resolved. Explain requirements and invite reapplication. |

### Overall Assessment

- Decision: ☐ APPROVED / ☐ CONDITIONALLY APPROVED / ☐ NOT APPROVED
- Conditions (if conditional): _______________
- Strengths: _______________
- Concerns: _______________
- First delivery date (if approved): _______________

### Sign-Off

| Role | Name | Date |
|------|------|------|
| Manager (assessed by) | _____ | _____ |
| Owner (approved by) | _____ | _____ |

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Supplier Questionnaire Not Sent Because of Time Pressure

**Why it happens:** The current bread supplier lets you down. You need bread tomorrow. A local bakery can deliver. The Manager places the order without completing the questionnaire because "we need bread for the morning."
**How to prevent:** Keep 1-2 backup suppliers pre-assessed on your register specifically for emergency situations. If you must use an unapproved supplier in a genuine emergency, place a single order and complete the questionnaire within 48 hours -- do not continue ordering without assessment.
**If it is already happening:** Identify the suppliers you are using without assessment. Complete the questionnaire for each one this week. If they cannot meet your standards, find alternatives.

### Risk 2: Supplier Provides Incomplete or Inaccurate Responses

**Why it happens:** A small supplier fills in the questionnaire quickly to get your business. They tick "Yes" to everything but cannot actually provide allergen specs or traceability records. Their answers are aspirational, not factual.
**How to prevent:** Request EVIDENCE for every "Yes." Do not accept a tick box -- ask for the actual certificate, the actual spec sheet, the actual insurance document. If they cannot provide evidence, the answer is "No" regardless of what they ticked.
**If it is already happening:** Go back to the supplier and request documentation for every "Yes" answer. If they cannot provide it within 2 weeks, downgrade the relevant score to RED.

### Risk 3: Approved Suppliers Never Reassessed After Initial Approval

**Why it happens:** The supplier was excellent when approved 3 years ago. Since then, they have changed ownership, moved premises, and altered their production processes. Nobody has checked whether they still meet the standards you approved.
**How to prevent:** Set an annual review date for every supplier (recorded on T015-01). At review, request updated certificates and ask about any changes. Combine with delivery temperature data from T002-03 to assess ongoing performance.
**If it is already happening:** Schedule reviews for all suppliers this quarter. Start with your highest-risk suppliers (those supplying chilled goods, allergen-containing products, or high volumes).

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Some of your suppliers may be very small operations (a local baker, a farm shop). They may not hold formal certifications. In this case, Area 1 scoring should focus on their Food Hygiene Rating and HACCP plan rather than BRC/SALSA. A Food Hygiene Rating of 5 plus a documented HACCP plan is sufficient for a small supplier.
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand likely has a central approved supplier list. Use this questionnaire for any local or supplementary suppliers not on the brand list.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** You may have fewer suppliers but they still need assessment. Even your milk and coffee bean suppliers should be on the register with documentation on file.
**Bakery Cafe:** Pay particular attention to Area 2 (Allergen Management) for ingredient suppliers. Flour, chocolate, and nut suppliers need especially thorough allergen documentation.

**Making it yours:**
- Adjust the scoring thresholds to match your risk tolerance
- Add questions specific to your product needs (e.g., organic certification, vegan certification)
- Consider visiting high-risk suppliers in person for their first assessment
- Keep completed questionnaires filed alongside the Approved Supplier Register

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T015-01 -- Approved Supplier Register (L1, tracker)
- T015-02 -- Delivery Inspection Checklist (L1, checklist)
- T015-04 -- Supplier Due Diligence SOP (L2, SOP)
- T015-05 -- Quarterly Supplier Performance Review (L3, scorecard)

**Related blocks:**
- B013

**Parent block:** B015 -- Supplier Due Diligence
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
